issue.title: GitClaw channel poll E2E 20260601T144151Z
issue.body:
@gitclaw /channels poll e2e-slack-route,e2e-telegram-route --poll-id poll-20260601T144151Z --message-id poll-msg-20260601T144151Z

Question: Which tiny channel feature should GitClaw ship for NOECHO_CHANNEL_POLL_QUESTION_20260601T144151Z?
Options:
- Ship the tiny poll
- Keep the huddle only
issue.author: AnandChowdhary

GitClaw created or reused a GitHub poll issue, then queued one provider-facing poll invitation per reviewed route. The poll issue contains the human-readable question and options; this source receipt keeps route names, poll IDs, question text, option text, thread IDs, message IDs, and outbound bodies out of band.

### Destinations
- destination=`01` target_issue=`#549` outbound_comment_id=`4593660434` target_issue_created=`true` duplicate_suppressed=`false` route_sha256_12=`ff6bd0e4b42c` channel=`slack` thread_id_sha256_12=`b88e1f401a7f` message_id_sha256_12=`08eccb483b25` body_sha256_12=`3bfb6f43b5f1`
- destination=`02` target_issue=`#550` outbound_comment_id=`4593660951` target_issue_created=`true` duplicate_suppressed=`false` route_sha256_12=`325d7a85c564` channel=`telegram` thread_id_sha256_12=`6b0770b7e315` message_id_sha256_12=`08eccb483b25` body_sha256_12=`3bfb6f43b5f1`

### Follow-Up Delivery
- provider gateways read pending poll invites with `gitclaw channel-outbox --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --out <file>`
- provider gateways record sent poll invites with `gitclaw channel-delivery --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --comment-id <comment> --external-message-id <message>`
- duplicate poll invites are suppressed independently for each route by `channel + message_id`
